intro: The clearest split is that Courier and Batch overlap on scope but take different approaches. Courier is Multi-channel notification API routing messages across email, push, SMS, Slack, and in-app with template; Batch is Mobile push notification and in-app messaging platform with segmentation, A/B testing, and transactional message. The data points Courier at mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging, and Batch at transactional + marketing push with behavioral segmentation and a/b testing push notification copy and send-time. Pricing: Courier is freemium, free tier; Batch is freemium, free tier. Lock-in is medium for Courier and medium for Batch, with DX at 4/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 4/5. Neither lists an enterprise tier, so contract-level buying may require a direct conversation. Honest trade-off: Courier is not for web-only products without mobile; Batch is not for teams needing email, sms, and in-app in one mature suite, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Courier is for mobile; Batch is for european mobile apps wanting gdpr-native push with segmentation; decide based on which scope-fit aligns with your team.
verdict_a: Choose Courier if your workload matches mobile and your work involves mobile app engagement. Skip it if your context is web-only products without mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Batch if your workload matches european mobile apps wanting gdpr-native push with segmentation and your work involves transactional + marketing push with behavioral segmentation sdk. Skip it if your context is teams needing email, sms, and in-app in one mature suite.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for Courier and unknown for Batch. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Courier to Batch?
faq.a: Courier is tagged with medium lock-in (proprietary routing, templates portable). Batch is tagged with medium lock-in (push/messaging sdk integration). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Courier or Batch?
faq.a: Both are rated 4/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Batch a good alternative to Courier?
faq.a: Batch is listed as fitting european mobile apps wanting gdpr-native push with segmentation, which overlaps with but is not identical to Courier's scope. Batch is flagged as not for teams needing email, sms, and in-app in one mature suite, so confirm your context does not fall there.
